title: Arbo-Watch Hub
section: Strengthening Arbovirus Surveillance, Analytics and Modelling Capacity in Africa
text:
Strengthening Arbovirus Surveillance, Analytics and Modelling Capacity in Africa
The Arbo-Watch Capacity Building Hub provides a comprehensive training programme designed to equip researchers, epidemiologists, public health practitioners, postgraduate students, and policymakers with practical skills in surveillance, statistical analysis, mathematical modelling, and spatial epidemiology.

title: Arbo-Watch Hub
text:
Programme Objectives
The programme aims to:

Strengthen arbovirus surveillance systems
Improve data management and quality assurance
Build capacity in statistical analysis
Develop mathematical modelling skills
Enhance spatial epidemiology expertise
Support evidence-based decision making

title: Arbo-Watch Hub
text:
Target Participants

Epidemiologists
Biostatisticians
Public Health Officers
Researchers
GIS Specialists
Graduate Students

title: Arbo-Watch Hub
text:
Learning Approach
The training combines:

Interactive lectures
Hands-on practical sessions
Case studies
Group projects
Real-world datasets
Collaborative learning

title: Arbo-Watch Hub
text:
Training Modules

Module I
Data Collection, Management and Analysis Tools


Module II
Introduction to R for Public Health Surveillance


Module III
Statistical Modelling for Epidemiological Research


Module IV
Mathematical Modelling Techniques: From Theory to Practice


Module V
Spatial Epidemiology, Risk Mapping and Decision Support Systems

title: Arbo-Watch Hub
text:
Expected Outcomes
Participants will be able to:

Design surveillance systems
Manage public health datasets
Conduct epidemiological analyses
Build disease transmission models
Produce risk maps
Develop evidence-informed recommendations

title: Training Curriculum
text:
# Curriculum Overview
The Arbo-Watch training programme consists of five integrated modules.



Module
Duration
Focus Area




I
5 Days
Data Collection and Management


II
5 Days
R Programming


III
5 Days
Statistical Modelling


IV
5 Days
Mathematical Modelling


V
5 Days
Spatial Epidemiology



## Training Philosophy
The curriculum emphasizes:

Practical application
Reproducible research
Open science
One Health approaches
Decision support

title: Facilitators
text:
Henry Athiany
Henry Athiany is a Lecturer in the Department of Statistics and Actuarial Sciences (STACS) at JKUAT, with training in Biostatistics and Epidemiology and a background in Mathematics and Computer Science. His research spans epidemiology, infectious disease modelling, Bayesian and spatiotemporal analysis, clinical trials, statistical computing, bioinformatics, time-series forecasting, and mental health analytics. With over 15 years of experience working with large-scale eHealth and routinely collected healthcare data, he has contributed to national public health initiatives, including serving on Kenya’s COVID-19 Modelling Technical Committee and supporting multiple Ministry of Health projects on digital health systems, diagnostics optimization, and health information interoperability. He is also an experienced trainer in statistical software and research methods and has held leadership roles within the International Biometric Society and the Statistical Society of Kenya. Currently, he supports the ARBO-WATCH project as part of the JKUAT team.

title: Module I
text:
This module equips participants with the knowledge and practical skills needed to collect, manage, and analyze data effectively using digital tools. Covers the entire data lifecycle, from planning and collection to storage, processing, and reporting, while emphasizing data quality, security, and ethical standards. Participants will gain hands-on experience with modern platforms such as Google Forms, Kobo Toolbox, and Microsoft Excel, enabling them to design digital data collection tools, conduct mobile-based field surveys, and perform basic to advanced data analysis for informed decision-making
